name: exifprobe
title: exifprobe
description:
read metadata from digital pictures
Exifprobe reads image files produced by digital cameras (including
several so-called "raw" file formats) and reports the structure of
the files and the auxiliary data and metadata contained within them.
.
In addition to TIFF, JPEG and EXIF, the program understands several
formats which may contain "raw" camera data, including MRW, CIFF/CRW,
JP2/JPEG2000, RAF, and X3F, as well as most TIFF-derived "raw" formats,
including DNG, ORF, CR2, NEF, K25/KDC/DCR and PEF.
.
This program is useful in forensics investigations.

commands.name: exifgrep
commands.description: select and reformat the output of exifprobe

arguments.description: turn on color output from exifprobe
arguments.raw: -c  turn on color output from exifprobe

arguments.description: ble shell (except that most shells will insist that the dots must be replaced, e.g.
arguments.raw: -compati  ble shell (except that most shells will insist that the dots must be replaced, e.g.

arguments.description: report in "variable" format, but with a Bourne-compatible export command
arguments.raw: -export  report in "variable" format, but with a Bourne-compatible export command

arguments.description: force exifprobe to print a filename for each line of its output, which exifgrep will
arguments.raw: -n  force exifprobe to print a filename for each line of its output, which exifgrep will

arguments.description: report numerical values rather than interpreted strings, for fields which have both.
arguments.raw: -num  report numerical values rather than interpreted strings, for fields which have both.

arguments.description: file arguments may be directories, which will be searched via find for all image
arguments.raw: -r  file arguments may be directories, which will be searched via find for all image

arguments.description: force exifprobe to print tag numbers for all itemes that have them, so that matches
arguments.raw: -t  force exifprobe to print tag numbers for all itemes that have them, so that matches

arguments.description: report in "variable" format, which may be directly `sourced' by any Bourne-compati-
arguments.raw: -var  report in "variable" format, which may be directly `sourced' by any Bourne-compati-

commands.name: exifprobe
commands.description: probe and report structure and metadata content of camera image files

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use exifgrep because read metadata from digital pictures.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use exifgrep as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `exifgrep [options] [egrep_options] egrep_pattern [NOT egrep-pattern] filename(s)` as documented upstream for exifgrep.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair exifgrep with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `exifgrep --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
